{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether schizophrenia patients with high intelligence constitute a clinically distinct sub-type of schizophrenia. The provided documents from MEDCORP and UMLS do not mention intelligence levels or any subtypes of schizophrenia based on cognitive ability. However, there is no reference to intelligence, cognitive performance, or a distinct subgroup of schizophrenia defined by high intelligence. The UMLS documents focus on organic psychosis, brain atrophy, repetitive movements, emotional blunting, and psychotic symptoms, but do not link these to intelligence levels.
